item.title:
Ranch belongs on a chicken cheesesteak ūüėé
Recipe below! I...
item.description:
Full post Embed


Ranch belongs on a chicken cheesesteak ūüėé Recipe below! Ingredients: -Chosen Foods Classic Ranch Dressing -1lb chicken tenders (optional to remove tendon) -2 bell peppers of choice (thinly sliced) -1 white onion (thinly sliced) -hoagie roll -sliced provolone cheese (or cheese of choice) -2-3tbsp Chosen Foods avocado oil -salt and pepper -garlic powder -Italian seasoning recipe: -slice bell peppers and onions. Add 1tbsp avocado oil to a skillet on medium heat. Add peppers and season with salt, pepper, garlic powder and Italian seasoning (measure with your heart!!). Stir to combine and saut√© until soft and slightly golden brown. Remove from pan or push aside -add more oil and chicken to the hot skillet, season with salt, pepper, and garlic powder. Flip and season again. Chop into small bite size pieces. Once fully cooked add veggies back and stir to combine. ***optional: to avoid needing to chop chicken, thinly slice it before adding to the pan. -separate into 3-4 different piles, top each pile with 2 slices of cheese. (Or put left overs in fridge if only making 1 sandwich) -once the cheese is melted, place hoagie roll on top. After 1 minute, flip over. Drizzle a generous amount of Chosen Foods ranch over the top! ENJOY!!!!
@Chosen Foods #ChosenPartner

item.title:
Dinner in 20 minutes and a sexy dishpic? Sign me up!!
Recipe...
item.description:
Full post Embed


Dinner in 20 minutes and a sexy dishpic? Sign me up!! Recipe, featuring new Knorr Flavor Base, below!! Ingredients: -2 tbsp minced garlic -1 tbsp dried Italian herbs -1 tbsp Knorr Chicken Flavor Base (divided) -1 lb boneless, skinless chicken thighs -1 tbsp plus 2tsp neutral oil -1 cup long grain white rice (rinsed) -2 cups water -1 pint grape tomatoes -4 oz baby spinach Recipe: -Combine garlic, dried herb blend and 2 tsp Knorr Chicken Flavor Base in bowl; rub mixture on chicken to coat completely -Heat 1 Tbsp oil in large skillet over medium-high heat and cook chicken, turning occasionally until browned, about 4 minutes. Remove to plate and set aside. -Heat remaining 2 tsp oil in same skillet and saut√© rice until lightly browned, about 1 minute. Add water and remaining Knorr Chicken Flavor Base and bring to a boil. -Stir in tomatoes and spinach. Place chicken on top; cover and continue cooking until the water is fully absorbed, rice is tender, and chicken is fully cooked, about 18 minutes. Let stand 5 minutes. ‚Ä®
@knorr #KnorrPartner

item.description:
Full post Embed


Back on my pesto kick ūüėé quick lunch with random things left over in the kitchen lmaooo Pesto ingredients: 1cup basil, 1/3c Parmesan,1/4c olive oil, 2 garlic cloves, 1 1/2tbsp pine nuts, 1tbsp lemon, salt

item.title: Ft. Sensor dust ‚ú® it‚Äôs a long unfortunate story. Anyways I c...
item.description:
Full post Embed


Ft. Sensor dust ‚ú® it‚Äôs a long unfortunate story. Anyways I could eat the entire dish, this was a 10/10 Ingredients: -18-20 jumbo shells -15oz ricotta cheese -2/3c grated parm -2/3c grated low moisture mozzarella -1 egg -1tbsp chopped basil, 1tsp dried oregano, chili flakes -salt, pepper -saut√©ed veggies: 2 diced squashes (I did zucchini and yellow, 1/2 white onion, kernels from 1 ear of corn, 3 minced garlic cloves) -25oz marinara sauce (I used summer lemon flavor! Highly recommend with this) -get a fun summer wine to pair with! I had a delish skin contact one :) Recipe: -on med heat add 1tbsp oil, onions, and salt. Saut√© until softened, add zucchini, corn and garlic and cook until slightly golden (5-10ish min) stir occasionally. Set aside and let cool slightly -cook jumbo noodles according to package (~14 min). Don‚Äôt undercook them, it doesn‚Äôt cook much in the sauce. Toss in oil after so they don‚Äôt stick together when assembling. -in a bowl mix together ricotta, mozzarella, Parmesan, egg, basil, oregano, chili flakes, and salt and pepper. Fold in saut√©ed veggies **I eyeballed this but the measurements above should get you to a good spot lmaoo you can‚Äôt really mess this up, if its too ‚Äúliquidy‚ÄĚ add more shredded cheese -preheat oven to 425F -pour sauce into a 9x16 dish, fill shells with about 2tbsp of the mixture and layer in the sauce. Cover in extra shredded cheese and foil. -Bake for 15 min, remove foil and bake for another 10 min until sauce and cheese is bubbling! -top with parm, lemon zest and basil ūüĆľ
@SAUZ

item.title:
Elote will be my go-to way to elevate corn this summer! ¬†
¬...
item.description:
Full post Embed


Elote will be my go-to way to elevate corn this summer! ¬† ¬†Prep, Cook and recrisped all in the Ninja Crispi¬†Recipe below! Ingredients: -2-4 ears of corn¬† -avocado oil -sauce: equal parts mayo and mexican crema (or sub sour cream), lime juice, grated garlic, salt (taste and adjust to your liking) -cotija cheese -paprika and ancho chili powder¬† -cilantro¬† Recipe:¬† -cover the corn in oil and salt. add to Ninja Crispi and airfry for 12-15 min, flip halfway. ****Conveniently chicken thighs cook for about the same amount of time, so I threw some in that had been marinating in a bbq mixture.¬† -while it's cooking, mix together sauce ingredients -once the corn has cooled slightly, brush on the sauce and cover in cotija cheese. sprinkle paprika and ancho chili powder, add cilantro!¬† -enjoy immediately, and thank me later!! :)¬† **store sauce, cotija cheese, and corn separately. When ready to eat the leftovers use the re-crisp option on the Ninja Crispi for 5-8 minutes. assemble elote and enjoy (again hehe)¬†
@Ninja Kitchen #NinjaPartner #NinjaCrispi
